metadata.framework: Agentic AI Governance Framework for Multimodal Prompt Injection Detection
metadata.description: Priority-ordered governance rules for insurance document LLM pipelines. Rules are grounded in OWASP LLM Top 10 2025 and NIST AI RMF. All thresholds tuned on validation set — never on test set.

decision_system.BLOCK: Document is rejected. Not passed to the insurance LLM under any circumstance.
decision_system.SANITIZE: Detected injection spans are redacted ([REDACTED]) before passing to LLM.
decision_system.ALLOW: Document passes all checks and is forwarded to the insurance LLM.

rules.name: Critical Risk Unconditional Block
rules.description: Any document with risk_score in CRITICAL range is blocked regardless of other signals. This is a hard policy boundary — no ML uncertainty can override it.
rules.reason_template: BLOCKED by rule G1 (Critical Risk Unconditional Block). Risk score {risk_score:.3f} exceeds critical threshold (≥0.80). Per OWASP LLM01:2025, high-confidence prompt injection attempts must be rejected before reaching the LLM. No further processing permitted.

rules.name: Hidden Text Detection Block
rules.description: Documents with confirmed hidden text (near-white text on white background) are always blocked. Hidden text is a steganographic attack pattern — it survives naive OCR and is designed to be invisible to human reviewers.
rules.policy_ref: OWASP LLM01:2025 — Indirect Prompt Injection; ISO 27001:2022 A.8.22
rules.reason_template: BLOCKED by rule G2 (Hidden Text Detection). Near-white text detected in document image — a steganographic injection technique invisible to human reviewers. This attack pattern falls under OWASP LLM01:2025 Indirect Prompt Injection. Risk score: {risk_score:.3f}. Vision score: {vision_score:.3f}.

rules.name: High Keyword Density Block
rules.description: Documents with very high injection keyword density combined with elevated prompt score are blocked. Keyword density > 0.40 with prompt score > 0.85 indicates a direct instruction-override attack.
rules.reason_template: BLOCKED by rule G3 (High Keyword Density). Injection keyword density {keyword_density:.3f} exceeds threshold (≥0.40) combined with high RoBERTa malicious probability {prompt_score:.3f} (≥0.85). Pattern consistent with direct instruction-override attack per OWASP LLM01:2025.

rules.name: Watermark Attack Block
rules.description: Documents where significant new text appears only at high contrast (watermark injection pattern) combined with elevated risk are blocked. Watermark score > 0.50 with risk > 0.65 indicates a low-opacity hidden instruction attack.
rules.reason_template: BLOCKED by rule G4 (Watermark Attack Detection). {watermark_score:.1%} of text only visible at high contrast — consistent with low-opacity watermark injection. Risk score: {risk_score:.3f}. This technique evades standard OCR and targets indirect injection per OWASP LLM01:2025.

rules.name: Sensitive Data Exfiltration Attack Block
rules.description: Documents with HIGH risk and elevated vision score where the attack family is predicted as data exfiltration are always blocked. Data exfiltration attempts to extract policyholder PII via LLM — catastrophic if successful.
rules.policy_ref: OWASP LLM06:2025 — Sensitive Information Disclosure; NIST AI RMF: GOVERN 1.7
rules.policy_source: OWASP LLM Top 10 2025 + NIST AI RMF
rules.reason_template: BLOCKED by rule G5 (Data Exfiltration Attack). Predicted attack family '{attack_family}' combined with risk score {risk_score:.3f} and vision score {vision_score:.3f} indicates a sensitive information disclosure attempt. Per OWASP LLM06:2025 and NIST AI RMF GOVERN 1.7, exfiltration attempts must be blocked to protect policyholder PII.

rules.name: Excessive Agency / Claim Manipulation Block
rules.description: Documents with high risk where the attack is classified as claim manipulation are blocked. Successful claim manipulation could result in fraudulent approvals or denials — a direct financial and regulatory risk.
rules.policy_ref: OWASP LLM08:2025 — Excessive Agency; IRDAI AML Guidelines
rules.reason_template: BLOCKED by rule G6 (Claim Manipulation). Predicted attack family '{attack_family}' with risk score {risk_score:.3f} indicates an attempt to manipulate insurance claim outcomes. This falls under OWASP LLM08:2025 (Excessive Agency) and IRDAI anti-fraud guidelines. Financial integrity risk classified as CRITICAL.

rules.name: High Risk + Footer Injection Sanitize
rules.description: Documents with HIGH risk and suspicious footer content undergo sanitization. Footer injections are often legitimate documents with appended malicious instructions — the document body may still be valid.
rules.reason_template: SANITIZE by rule G7 (Footer Injection). {footer_density:.1%} of text boxes located in footer region (≥30%) with risk score {risk_score:.3f}. Footer region will be redacted before LLM processing. Document body retained for claim processing. Per OWASP LLM01:2025 indirect injection mitigation.

rules.name: Medium Risk Multimodal Sanitize
rules.description: Documents with medium-HIGH risk score from the fusion model, or with elevated vision score, are sanitized. Both text and image signals are suspicious but not definitively malicious.
rules.policy_ref: OWASP LLM01:2025; NIST AI RMF: MAP 5.1
rules.policy_source: OWASP LLM Top 10 2025 + NIST AI RMF
rules.reason_template: SANITIZE by rule G8 (Medium Multimodal Risk). Fusion risk score {risk_score:.3f} and vision score {vision_score:.3f} exceed dual thresholds. Detected injection spans will be redacted ([REDACTED]) before LLM processing. Human review recommended. Per NIST AI RMF MAP 5.1.

rules.name: Elevated Prompt Score Sanitize
rules.description: Documents where RoBERTa gives elevated malicious probability (0.55-0.80) but risk model is below block threshold undergo sanitization. Captures model uncertainty range.
rules.reason_template: SANITIZE by rule G9 (Elevated Prompt Score). RoBERTa malicious probability {prompt_score:.3f} is elevated (≥0.55) but below block threshold. Conservative sanitization applied — injection-like text spans redacted before LLM processing.

rules.name: Critical Severity Metadata Flag
rules.description: Documents marked with critical severity in metadata that also have non-trivial vision signals are sanitized as a precaution.
rules.reason_template: SANITIZE by rule G10 (Critical Severity Metadata). Document severity is '{severity}' with non-trivial visual signals (vision_score={vision_score:.3f}). Precautionary sanitization applied per NIST AI RMF GOVERN 6.1 (high-stakes automated decision review).
